title: Gentianopsis doluchanovii
taxonomy: Gentianopsis doluchanovii is a species of flowering plant in the gentian family (Gentianaceae). It belongs to the genus Gentianopsis, which was previously considered part of the genus Gentianella. The plant is native to Central Asia and China. The classification of Gentianopsis doluchanovii is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the phylum Angiospermophyta. Its classification is an ongoing area of study in the field of botany.

intro: Gentianopsis doluchanovii, also known as Doluchanov's gentian or Russian gentian, is a species of flowering plant in the gentian family, characterized by its small, blue or white flowers and grass-like foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Gentianopsis doluchanovii include iridoid glycosides, such as gentiopicrin and swertiamarin, which are believed to be responsible for the plant's medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a variety of health conditions, including stomach problems and bronchial infections.
